channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: A 'normal guy' doing 'brilliant' things - Lammens on life at Man Utd
item.description: Manchester United goalkeeper Senne Lammens says he is "just a normal guy", but his reputation is growing in an impressive debut season at Old Trafford.

item.title: How have Chelsea made biggest loss in English football history?
item.description: Chelsea recorded a pre-tax loss of £355m for 2024-25 - the highest loss ever made by an English football club - according to financial figures released by Uefa. How did it happen?

item.title: Fans of richest PL clubs pay £74 per match as ticket revenue soars
item.description: Fans of the Premier League's six wealthiest clubs are paying an average of £74 per ticket for each match they attend, while ticket revenues are soaring, according to data from a new report.

item.title: Spurs relegation fight toughest of career - Tudor
item.description: Tottenham interim manager Igor Tudor says trying to save the club from Premier League relegation is "probably" the toughest challenge of his career.

item.title: Brentford boss Andrews signs new deal to 2032
item.description: Brentford head coach Keith Andrews signs a new contract with the club, running until 2032.

item.title: How Rosenior's 'son' Sarr got Chelsea call after 'emotional' Afcon
item.description: Senegal's 20-year-old defender Mamadou Sarr is pushing for a first Premier League start when Chelsea face Arsenal after a rapid rise under Liam Rosenior.

item.title: 'He was always going to score' - Real beat Benfica to reach last 16
item.description: Watch as Real Madrid head through to the last 16 in the Champions League after a 2-1 victory against Benfica.

item.title: 'The portfolio keeps expanding' - Ronaldo becomes Almeria co-owner
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo becomes part-owner of Spanish second division side Almeria after acquiring a 25% share in the Saudi Arabian-owned club.

item.title: Real Madrid condemn fan for alleged Nazi salute
item.description: Real Madrid launch an investigation after a supporter allegedly performed a Nazi salute before the club's Champions League win against Benfica on Wednesday.

item.title: Handling of Man City case damaging for Premier League - Tebas
item.description: The Premier League has been "damaged" by the fact there is yet to be a verdict in the case against Man City, says Javier Tebas.

item.title: Galatasaray score twice in extra time to oust 10-man Juventus
item.description: Galatasaray survived a 10-man Juventus comeback, scoring twice in extra time to win 7-5 on aggregate, despite Lloyd Kelly's red card.

item.title: PSG avoid late scare against 10-man Monaco
item.description: PSG held on despite a late Jordan Teze goal, after Marquinhos and Khvicha Kvaratskhelia scored in quick succesion to seal the tie for French champions, as Mamadou Coulibaly was sent off for Monaco.

item.title: Reckless or unlucky? Did Juventus' Kelly deserve red card?
item.description: Watch as Steven Warnock, Rory Smith, and Nedum Onuoha analyse the events leading to Juventus' Lloyd Kelly receiving a red card on Match of the Day with Mark Chapman.

item.title: Atalanta score dramatic late penalty to knock out Dortmund
item.description: Atalanta score a penalty with the last kick of the game, as the Italians stage an incredible fightback to beat Borussia Dortmund and progress to the last 16 of the Champions League.

item.title: Brilliant Bodo/Glimt shock Inter to reach last 16
item.description: Bodo/Glimt produced one of the biggest upsets in Champions League history as they knocked out last year's finalists Inter Milan to reach the last 16 for the first time.

item.title: Pick Ekitike, Sesko and Van Dijk - FPL team of the week
item.description: It is a good week for Fantasy Premier League managers to load up on Liverpool and Manchester United players, write Thomas Woods.

item.title: Arsenal's Smith out of Canada squad with concussion
item.description: Arsenal forward Olivia Smith withdraws from the Canada squad for the SheBelieves Cup because of concussion.

item.title: 'Clock is ticking' on Premier League & EFL stand-off, says regulator
item.description: The "clock is ticking" for the Premier League and EFL to end their financial impasse, says the chair of the Independent Football Regulator.

item.title: Edwards and Le Bris condemn racist abuse of players
item.description: Wolves manager Rob Edwards and Sunderland counterpart Regis Le Bris condemn the racist abuse their players suffered last weekend.

item.title: What do West Brom do next with relegation looming?
item.description: After the end of Eric Ramsay's winless 44 days in charge at West Bromwich Albion, what happens next for the struggling Championship club?

item.title: Antonio set to join Qatar's Al‑Sailiya after Charlton talks
item.description: Michail Antonio is set to sign a short-term deal with Qatari club Al‑Sailiya before his return to club football following a near-fatal car crash.

item.title: Title talk 'irrelevant' for Motherwell's Askou
item.description: Motherwell manager Jens Berthel Askou says talk of his side being in the race to win the Scottish Premiership is "irrelevant". But could his side win the league?

item.title: Newcastle sweep aside Qarabag in Champions League
item.description: Newcastle progress to the Round of 16 in the Champions League, after comfortably beating Qarabag 3-2 (9-3 aggregate) in the knockout phase play-offs.

item.title: Warnock questions Real Madrid's 'defensive mentality'
item.description: Watch as Steven Warnock, Rory Smith, and Nedum Onuoha break down how Real Madrid's defence are vulnerable, on Match of the Day with Mark Chapman.

item.title: Sorloth hat-trick for Atletico seals last-16 spot
item.description: Alexander Sorloth's hat-trick sees Atletico Madrid progress to the Round of 16, after 4-1 win against Club Brugge (7-4 on aggregate). Sorloth becomes only the second player in Atletico's history to net a treble in the competition since 2014.

item.title: Man City, Atletico, now Inter - how Bodo/Glimt got to last 16
item.description: Watch Bodo/Glimt "tear up the European established order" as they make their way to the Champions League last 16, defeating giants like Manchester City, Atletico Madrid, and Inter Milan on the way.

item.title: Leverkusen advance to face Bayern or Arsenal in last 16
item.description: Bayer Leverkusen advance in the Champions League after a 0-0 draw against Olympiakos. The result was enough to secure a 2-0 aggregate victory, following their first-leg win in Greece. Leverkusen will face either Arsenal or Bayern Munich in the Round of 16.

item.title: Who is most affected by WSL Asian Cup exodus?
item.description: As the Asian Cup gets under way on Sunday, BBC Sport assesses its impact on Women's Super League sides.

item.title: Why Villa are this season's big overperformers
item.description: Are Aston Villa wobbling?

item.title: Bizarre VAR? Why Kelly's second yellow card became 'awful' straight red
item.description: An 'awful' decision, or 'always going to be a red'? Why Juventus' Lloyd Kelly's second yellow card became a straight red in his side's Champions League exit at the hands of Galatasaray.

item.title: 'Easy from afar' - no Foxes flak for Rowett comments
item.description: Gary Rowett says he has not had to clear the air with Leicester's hierarchy about criticism he aimed their way just weeks before his appointment.

item.title: Real Madrid win 'for everyone who stands against racism'
item.description: Real Madrid's win against Benfica was a "victory for everyone who stands against racism", midfielder Aurelien Tchouameni says.

item.title: UK's youngest senior footballer switches allegiance
item.description: Chelsea academy player and former Glenavon midfielder Chris Atherton switches his international allegiance from Northern Ireland to the Republic of Ireland.

item.title: Salah happy to stay at Liverpool - Thursday's gossip
item.description: Mohamed Salah would be happy to stay at Liverpool until 2027, there are six Premier League clubs interested in Frankfurt's Nnamdi Collins, while Casemiro wants to keep playing in Europe.

item.title: Why is it all going wrong at Walsall again?
item.description: A split fanbase, questions over style of play and no home win since Boxing Day, it has become another winter of discontent at Walsall.

item.title: 'I'm ready' - Park bids to show Man Utd form for England
item.description: Manchester United midfielder Jess Park has been in brilliant form since her move in the summer - but can she take that into England's upcoming matches?

item.title: 'Darlings of Italian football' - how Atalanta salvaged national pride
item.description: As Italy faced the embarrassment of having no clubs in the Champions League last 16, Atalanta became the saviours of national reputation - and pride.

item.title: 'Really ugly' abuse affects players - Wales' Cain
item.description: Wales international Hannah Cain says the mental health of female players can be affected by "really ugly" social media abuse.

item.title: Super-sub Sesko earns Man Utd win at Everton
item.description: Watch Premier League highlights as Manchester United earn a narrow win over Everton thanks to a goal from substitute Benjamin Sesko.

item.title: Will EFL Cup final affect Premier League title race?
item.description: Mark Chapman, Theo Walcott and Chris Sutton discuss the potential impact of the Carabao Cup final on the Premier League title race.

item.title: Snoop Dogg meets the Swans
item.description: Legendary rapper Snoop Dogg paid a visit to the Swansea City training ground the day before watching the club for the first time since becoming an investor.

item.title: Carrick praises 'ruthless' goalscorer Sesko
item.description: Manchester United manager Michael Carrick praises the impact of striker Benjamin Sesko, who came off the bench to score in a 1-0 win at Everton.

item.title: Has Arteta solved the Gyokeres problem?
item.description: Umir Irfan analyses how Mikel Arteta got the best out of Viktor Gyokeres in the win over Tottenham and how Arsenal have not always played to the striker's strengths this season.

item.title: Scotland fans secure Fifa approval to wear sporrans at World Cup
item.description: Thousands of supporters are expected to travel to the US for Scotland's first appearance in the tournament since 1998.

item.title: Man Utd post profit but debt rises to £1.3bn
item.description: The amount Manchester United owe increases to almost £1.3bn in outstanding transfer fees, rolling credit facility and legacy debt.

item.title: When is Champions League last-16 draw and how does it work?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at when the Champions League knockout phase draw takes place and who Premier League teams could face.

item.title: Bord consortium pull out of Sheff Wed takeover
item.description: A consortium led by former professional poker player James Bord pulls out of a takeover of Sheffield Wednesday

item.title: England to host Ukraine at Everton's Hill Dickinson
item.description: The Lionesses will host Ukraine in their Women's World Cup qualifier at the Hill Dickinson Stadium in June.

item.title: Everton worthy of more than a defeat - Moyes
item.description: Everton manager David Moyes says his side were "worth more than a defeat" after losing 1-0 to Manchester United in the Premier League.

item.title: Saka and Timber partnership among 'best in Europe'
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Danny Murphy looks at how the combination of Bukayo Saka and Jurrien Timber down the right side tormented Tottenham's defence in Arsenal's 4-1 win over their north London rivals.

item.title: Spurs' league position is a 'big concern' - Rooney
item.description: Wayne Rooney discusses the possibility of Tottenham Hotspur getting relegated this season.

item.title: Referee disallowed Spurs goal for 'soft' push - Given
item.description: Match of the Day pundits Shay Given and Danny Murphy believe Tottenham "have a reason to be disappointed" with Randal Kolo Muani's second 'goal' being ruled out for a foul on Arsenal defender Gabriel.

item.title: Gyokeres performance 'incredible' - Arteta
item.description: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta is full of praise for Viktor Gyokeres' "outstanding" performance in the Gunners 4-1 win over Tottenham. The Swedish striker bagged a brace to help his side go five points clear at the top of the Premier League.

item.title: Courting controversy and befriending Trump - Fifa's 'unlikely saviour'
item.description: Gianni Infantino has clashed with unions and European football, but after 10 years as Fifa president he remains in an impregnable position.

item.title: Why Champions League return is crucial to Man Utd
item.description: Up to fourth in the Premier League after Monday's win at Everton, Champions League qualification would be a big boost to Manchester United's finances after two years outside Europe's elite club competition.

item.title: New stadium curse - are Everton showing how clubs struggle after move?
item.description: As Everton's home form continues to struggle, BBC Sport takes a closer look at the curse of the new stadium in the Premier League era.

item.title: Can you name the all-time top Champions League goalscorers?
item.description: We want you to name every player who has scored at least 25 goals in the Champions League or the European Cup.

item.title: The former kitman, 102, who left his mark on the World Cup
item.description: Charlie O'Leary was the Republic of Ireland kitman, but his influence extends to football across the island.

item.title: 'Losing the dressing room' - what does it mean and how can it happen?
item.description: "Losing the dressing room" is now part of football's vocabulary. Chief football writer Phil McNulty asks what it means, how it happens and can it be solved?

item.title: All February's done deals, including deadline day, in one place
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: What is St Totteringham's Day and when could it happen?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains what St Totteringham's Day is and what its origins are

item.title: VAR vs TMO – what are the key differences?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the key differences between football's video assistant referees and rugby union's use of television match officials.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League appearances?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything Team look at which players have the highest total Premier League appearances.

item.title: Where do iconic football moves originate from?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team explain where the names of iconic football moves come from.

item.title: Why is there a four-week break in the WSL?
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look at why there is a second hiatus in the Women's Super League so soon after the winter break.

item.title: Why are some football shin pads so small?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team explore why some football shin pads are so small, and what the laws are around wearing them.

item.title: How does the Nations League work and when is the draw?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how the Uefa Nations League works ahead of the draw for the 2026-27 competition

item.title: Thousands of Glasgow council staff to get World Cup holiday
item.description: Councillors were told the £520,000 cost of giving the extra day off will be met from balances.

item.title: Celtic linked with Plymouth's Tolaj & Pepple - gossip
item.description: Plymouth pair linked with Celtic as Hearts consider move for Rangers defender.

item.title: Injuries to Findlay, Ageu and Gordon hit Hearts
item.description: Scottish Premiership leaders Hearts will be without defender Stuart Findlay for six weeks while record signing Eduardo Ageu will miss the rest of the season.

item.title: Ferguson 'confident' of Rangers Old Firm derby win
item.description: Former Rangers captain and manager Barry Ferguson believes the Scottish Premiership title race is going to "go to the wire" but is "confident" of Old Firm victory over Celtic on Sunday.

item.title: Vinicius: Eight years at Real Madrid, 20 cases of alleged racist abuse
item.description: Spanish football expert Guillem Balague details how Vincius Jr has become a global symbol of resistance against discrimination.

item.title: Mourinho made 'unacceptable, huge mistake' - Kompany
item.description: Bayern Munich boss Vincent Kompany says Jose Mourinho made a "huge mistake" with his "unacceptable" post-match comments after the alleged racial abuse of Real Madrid winger Vinicius Jr.

item.title: 'Golazo!'- why one of football's most iconic intros has returned
item.description: BBC Sport looks at a feature of Serie A coverage which is delighting fans once more, three decades after the height of its popularity.

item.title: Kane reaches 500 goals - what other records could he break?
item.description: Bayern Munich striker Harry Kane became the first Englishman to reach 500 career goals on Saturday, but what other records could he break?

item.title: Football must avoid 'microscopic' VAR - Uefa refs' chief
item.description: Uefa referees' chief Roberto Rosetti says video assistant review (VAR) is becoming too forensic and handball must be revisited.

item.title: Co-founder leaves multi-club owners Mercury13
item.description: Multi-club ownership group co-founder Victoire Cogevina Reynal has left Mercury13 five months after taking over Bristol City Women.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?

item.title: When River Plate icon Diaz took over fourth-tier Oxford
item.description: Twenty years on from Ramon Diaz's surprising appointment at Oxford United, BBC Sport looks at what happened and how it went.
